data.title: Tollgate Operator Salik to Halt Trading in Dubai
data.description:
Salik (DFM:SALIK) shares will temporarily pause trading on the Dubai Financial Market from 12 pm local time on Wednesday due to its general assembly.

The tollgate operator will resume trading once the results of the meeting are disclosed, according to a Wednesday release.

data.title: KKR Nears $2.85 Billion Deal With EQT For E45 Maker Karo Healthcare, FT Reports, Citing Sources
data.description:
By Dominic Chopping

  • KKR is nearing a deal to buy E45 maker Karo Healthcare in a buyout that values the healthcare company at about 2.6 billion euros ($2.85 billion), Financial Times reports, citing unnamed sources.
  • The U.S. private equity group bested a rival bid from PAI Partners to clinch the company behind over-the-counter medicines such as athlete's foot treatment Lamisil and moisturizer E45, paying 2.65 billion euros including debt, the people said, according to FT.
  • Karo's current owner, EQT, struck a deal to take the business private in 2022, valuing it at about 1.4 billion euros. EQT first became a majority owner of Karo in 2019 when it was still a publicly-listed business.

-Karo, EQT and PAI declined to comment while KKR did not immediately respond to a request for comment, FT says.

Full story: https://tinyurl.com/4va2f8wt

Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com

data.title: Banca Monte dei Paschi Gets ECB Approval to Issue New Shares for Mediobanca Bid
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker

Banca Monte dei Paschi said it received approval from the European Central Bank to raise capital from the issuance of new shares as part of its bid for Mediobanca.

The Italian bank, formally called Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena, said late Tuesday that the new shares will be issued as common Equity tier 1 capital.

Shareholders of MPS are set to vote on the board's proposed capital increase for its all-share bid for its larger peer on April 17.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: New Zealand Stocks Finish Lower
data.description:
New Zealand's benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index closed 0.7% lower at 11,807 on Wednesday, mirroring declines in major global indexes amid worries that an intensifying trade war could lead to a global recession.

President Donald Trump’s sweeping tariffs took effect today, including massive 104% duties on China—New Zealand’s biggest export market.

Domestically, the Reserve Bank of New Zealand cut its official cash rate by 25bps to 3.5% as widely expected, amid a steady decline in inflation and weakening domestic economic conditions.

The RBNZ also cautioned about downside risks to the local economy from global trade barriers.

Nearly all sectors on the index finished in the red.

Among large-cap stocks, notable losses were seen from Fisher & Paykel (-1.6%), Infratil (-1.6%), Mercury NZ (-1.4%), and Spark NZ (-1.7%).

Other key decliners included Port of Tauranga (-2.6%), Fletcher Building (-1.3%), and Summerset Group (-1.8%).

data.title: Long-End U.S. Treasury Yields JumpMarket Talk
data.description: Long-dated U.S. Treasury yields jump in overnight trade, where volumes are likely thinner, with the 10-year yield hitting its highest level since late February. The 10-year Treasury yield rose to 4.511% before easing back to 4.407%, still up 14.5 bps, according to Tradeweb. The 30-year Treasury yield jumps 16.5 bps to 4.879%, having earlier touched 5%, according to Tradeweb. The upward correction continues as investors try to cope with the elevated level of market volatility. "What really worries me right now, though, is the action that we see across the Treasury complex, where there's been no let-up whatsoever in selling pressure at the long end of the curve," Pepperstone's senior research strategist Michael Brown says in a note. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Indian Rupee opens lower against the US dollar
data.description: In an early morning trade on Wednesday, the Indian rupee opened 20 paise lower against the US dollar at 85.47, amid weaker domestic equity markets.The domestic currency previously closed at 85.27 against the U.S. dollar.Meanwhile this morning, the Sensex opened lower by 364.27 points or 0.49 percent at 73,862.81, and the Nifty was down 115.15 points or 0.51 percent at 22,420.70

data.title: Roehm announces hike in April MMA and GMAA prices in the US
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in the U.S. informed a team member that Roehm has increased prices of all methyl methacrylate (MMA) and glacial methacrylic acid (GMAA) grades by 5 cents/lb (USD 110/mt), wef April 1, 2025 to be sold in the U.S.

data.title: Market Chatter: BOJ Faces Pressure As US Targets Weak Yen In Tariff Dispute
data.description:
US-Japan talks on tariffs may pressure the Bank of Japan to raise rates sooner, as Washington links Japan's weak yen to trade imbalances, Nikkei reported Wednesday.

The talks, led by US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, follow a 24% tariff on Japanese goods. Trump has tied currency levels to trade fairness, while Tokyo denies pursuing yen weakness, according to the report.

Market odds of a BOJ hike in June or July have dropped but could rise if the weak yen becomes central to negotiations, the report said.

A potential Ishiba-Trump meeting during the June G7 summit may coincide with the BOJ's next policy review, according to the report.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Castellum President, CEO to Resign in 2026
data.description:
Castellum said Wednesday that Joacim Sjoberg will resign as president and chief executive officer in 2026.

Sjoberg will remain with the Swedish real estate group until a successor is named. He will serve as a senior adviser to the company after vacating his office until Dec. 31, 2026, to ensure a smooth transition.

data.title: Poland - Factors to Watch April 9
data.description:
The following are significant news stories, press reports and events on Wednesday. ALL TIMES GMT (Poland: GMT + 2 hour):

SANTANDER BANK

Spain's Santander is exploring options for its roughly $8 billion (7.3 billion euro) majority stake in its Polish unit, Santander Bank Polska (SPL.WA), including a potential sale, Bloomberg News reported on Tuesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

TNT SUPPLY AGREEMENT

An agreement between the Polish Nitro-Chem and Paramount Enterprises International for the supply of TNT to the Polish army will be signed on Wednesday, the Ministry of Defence announced.

ELECTROMOBILITY

ElectroMobility Poland is abandoning its plans to create a Polish electric car, the Izera, and wants to create an electromobility hub, the center of which will be a car factory, not necessarily of a Polish brand, but manufactured in 80% from Polish components, Puls Biznesu daily reported.

DEBT

On Wednesday at 1000 the Ministry of Finance will hold a bond switch tender.

****Reuters has not verified stories reported by Polish media and does not vouch for their accuracy.****

data.title: Endesa Board Approves Second Tranche of Buyback Program
Ades Holding Extends Three Jackup Rig Operations Deals in Egypt
data.description:
Ades Holding Co. (SASE:2382) said Wednesday it secured extensions for three Egyptian offshore jackup rig operations contracts with General Petroleum Co.

The drilling services company's deals for the Admarine III and Admarine VI rigs were renewed for one year, with an option to extend by another year. The contracts will take effect in 2025 and 2026.

Meanwhile, the Admarine V rig contract was extended for three years, with a one-year extension option, effective immediately.

data.title: India's gold loan companies slump as RBI proposes tighter regulations
data.description:
** Shares of Muthoot Finance tank 10%, IIFL Finance slide 7% and Manappuram Finance dip 3%

** In its monetary policy review on Wednesday, India's central bank said it will to issue comprehensive regulations on prudential norms and conduct-related aspects for gold loan providers

** Reuters reported last month that the Reserve Bank of India plans to ask lenders to follow stricter underwriting processes for gold loans

** This is MUTT's worst one-day pct drop since August 2022, IIFL's worst pct fall since March 2024

** MUTT down 3.2% YTD, IIFL down 24.4% YTD.

** YTD, MNFL up 18%

data.title: Dubai Insurance to Halt Trading in Dubai
data.description:
Dubai Insurance (DFM:DIN) shares will temporarily pause trading on the Dubai Financial Market from 11 am local time on Wednesday due to its general assembly.

The Dubai-based insurer will resume trading once the results of the meeting are disclosed, according to a Wednesday release.

data.title: Chemanol Appoints Adviser to Probe Addar, Global Co. Deals
data.description:
Methanol Chemicals Co. (SASE:2001), d/b/a Chemanol, enlisted a specialized third-party adviser to carry out a forensic audit of its acquisitions of Addar Chemicals and Global Co. for Chemical Industries.

The decision was made after a recommendation from the chemical company's audit committee, according to a Wednesday filing.

Chemanol noted that the acquisitions were carried out during the term of its previous board.

data.title: Endesa: Second Tranche of Buyback to Start Wednesday, End No Later Than Dec. 31
Copper Holds Decline as New US Tariffs Kick In
data.description:
Copper futures held a recent decline to around $4.1 per pound on Wednesday, hovering near multi-month lows amid growing concerns that an escalating trade war could trigger a global recession.

US President Donald Trump’s sweeping reciprocal tariffs came into effect today, including a hefty 104% levy on Chinese goods.

Beijing recently vowed to “fight to the end” and pledged to escalate its retaliation on US exports.

The lack of concrete developments on tariff negotiations also weighed on investor sentiment, although Trump indicated that he remains open to discussions with major trading partners.

Copper prices were pressured further as the new US tariffs exclude copper, gold, energy, and certain critical minerals not sourced domestically, despite previous reports that the metal could be targeted within weeks.

data.title: Market Chatter: Japan Seeks Tariff Reprieve as Stocks Slide, Yen Gains
data.description:
Japanese officials arrived in Washington for talks as Tokyo warned of economic fallout from US tariffs, Bloomberg reported Wednesday.

Finance Minister Katsunobu Kato said the levies could hurt trade and sentiment, while Economic Revitalization Minister Ryosei Akazawa vowed to act in the national interest, according to the report.

The Topix and Nikkei 225 fell over 3% as the yen strengthened and tariffs took effect. BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda struck a cautious tone, with traders scaling back rate hike bets, the report said.

US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Trade Representative Jamieson Greer will lead the American side, according to the report.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Santander Exploring Options to Sell Polish Unit Stake, Bloomberg Says, Citing Sources
data.description:
• Banco Santander is exploring a possible sale of its majority share in its Polish arm as it focuses on expansion in the Americas, Bloomberg reports, citing unnamed sources.
  • The Spanish bank is working with an adviser to review its 62% holding in Santander Bank Polska and has reached out to potential investors, according to Bloomberg.
  • Santander declined to comment, Bloomberg said.

data.title: PRESSR: EMSTEEL recognised as 2025 ‘worldsteel sustainability champion’ for second consecutive year
data.description:
Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates: EMSTEEL (“the Group”), one of the largest publicly traded steel and building materials manufacturers in the region, has announced its recognition as a 2025 Steel Sustainability Champion by the World Steel Association (worldsteel), cementing the Group’s position as the sole receiver of the prestigious accolade in the Middle East and North African region for a second consecutive year.

The recognition was presented during the worldsteel Special General Meeting held in Sydney, Australia. Now in its eighth year, the Steel Sustainability Champion accolade—part of The Steelie Awards—honours companies demonstrating exceptional commitment to sustainable development in the global steel industry.

The recognition is a direct result of the significant strides EMSTEEL made throughout 2024 in enhancing sustainability. A groundbreaking initiative that stood out was the company’s partnership with Masdar to launch the MENA region’s first green hydrogen demonstration project for the steel industry, positioning EMSTEEL as a leader in the UAE’s hydrogen economy and a driving force for providing sustainable solutions for the construction and manufacturing sectors.

Beyond its national initiatives, EMSTEEL is also making a significant global impact. As a co-chair of the Alliance for Industry Decarbonisation, an initiative led by the International Renewable Energy Agency, EMSTEEL is working alongside 84 partners to set joint decarbonisation targets including tripling renewable energy production and reducing greenhouse gas emissions by 54% by 2030.

As part of its ambitious decarbonisation roadmap, EMSTEEL aims to reduce its greenhouse gas emissions by 40% in its Steel Business Unit and 30% in its Cement Business by 2030, with the ultimate goal of achieving net-zero emissions by 2050.

The company has already made substantial progress, having reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions intensity by 23%. As of 2023, EMSTEEL’s total Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions stood at 4.5 million tonnes of CO₂, marking a significant step forward in its journey toward net-zero.

Engineer Saeed Ghumran Al Remeithi, Group CEO of EMSTEEL, said: “Being named a worldsteel Sustainability Champion is a true honour and a reflection of the dedication and hard work of everyone at EMSTEEL. It reinforces our commitment to driving the transition towards a low-carbon future, not just for the steel industry, but for the entire global industrial landscape. With a continued focus on innovation, efficiency, and environmental stewardship, we will build a cleaner, more sustainable tomorrow.”

He added: “As the first steelmaker in the world to capture part of its CO₂ emissions, we have been able to operate with 45% less carbon intensity than the global average and are excited to continue playing a cataclysmic role in decarbonising this hard-to-abate sector and the downstream supply chain.”

The Steel Sustainability Champions Programme, administered by worldsteel, recognises companies that fulfil stringent criteria, including signing the worldsteel Sustainability Charter, meeting 20 sustainability criteria, providing Life Cycle Inventory (LCI) data, and participating in worldsteel’s initiatives such as the Steelie Awards or the Safety and Health Recognition Programme.

About EMSTEEL

EMSTEEL is a public joint stock company and the UAE’s largest steel and building materials manufacturer. The Group leverages cutting-edge technologies to supply both the local market and over 70 international markets with high-quality finished products, creating a one-stop shop for the manufacturing and construction sectors.

EMSTEEL is committed to contributing to the UAE's industrial strategy ‘Operation 300 billion’ by delivering market-leading products to support local industries, creating job opportunities for UAE Nationals, and enhancing its sustainable practices. The Group is a global leader in low-carbon steel production and is aligned with the UAE's Net Zero by 2050 Strategic Initiative.

Headquartered in Abu Dhabi, EMSTEEL operates 16 state-of-the-art plants, with a production capacity of 3.5 million tonnes of steel and 4.6 million tonnes of cement annually, fueling the nation’s most iconic projects.

EMSTEEL is majority owned by ADQ, one of the region’s largest holding companies with a broad portfolio of major enterprises spanning key sectors of Abu Dhabi’s diversified economy.

For more information, please visit our website: www.emsteel.com.

Send us your press releases to pressrelease.zawya@lseg.com

data.title: New Zealand Shares Fall Amid Continued US-China Trade War; Tourism Holdings to Relocate Portion of US Rental Fleet to Canada
data.description:
New Zealand shares fell on Wednesday as global markets started to digest President Donald Trump's aggressive 104% tariff on China.

The S&P/NZX 50 Index fell 0.71% or 84.89 points to close at 11,806.55.

Recession fears have intensified globally as the US and China refuse to back away from a trade war that has disturbed global trading.

Tariff revenues will help narrow the US budget deficit for the year, but the hit to economic growth and additional tax cuts are likely to limit the size of any lasting fiscal benefit, Fitch Ratings said in a Wednesday statement.

In the local economy, the Reserve Bank of New Zealand cuts the official cash rate by 25 basis points to 3.5%.

"The recently announced increases in global trade barriers weaken the outlook for global economic activity. On balance, these developments create downside risks to the outlook for economic activity and inflation in New Zealand," the central bank said.

Also, New Zealand's national residential property values rose by an average of 0.2% in the three months to March, according to the latest QV House Price Index.

In corporate news, Tourism Holdings is relocating a portion of its existing US rental fleet to Canada ahead of the tariff implementation.

Scales said the impact of US tariffs on earnings is currently expected to be minimal and there is no change to current earnings guidance for the fiscal year.

data.title: Taiwan's Taiex Ends 5.8% Lower at 17391.76
Comcast NBCUniversal to Open Theme Park in Bedford, U.K.
data.description:
By Ian Walker

Comcast NBCUniversal plans to build a theme park in Bedford, U.K., its first ever resort complex in Europe.

The media and entertainment company said the park will be designed by its Universal Destinations & Experiences segment. It will have several themed lands with rides, attractions and entertainment. The resort will also have a 500-room hotel and a retail, dining and entertainment complex.

Universal said it plans to submit a planning application for the park soon and if approved plans to start construction on the site next year. It bought the 476-acre piece of land in Bedford in 2023.

"Bringing the Universal brand to the United Kingdom is another exciting step forward in generating future growth," said Mark Woodbury, Chairman and CEO of Universal Destinations & Experiences.

The U.K. government said Wednesday that the park will create around 28,000 jobs and boost the economy by 50 billion pounds ($63.83 billion). The park is expected to open in 2031.

Write to Ian Walker at ian.walker@wsj.com
